title: Hybrid Retrieval, Score Calibration, and Rank Fusion · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A county benefits service and community accessibility panel. They need authoritative effective-dated policy ingestion, multilingual exact and semantic search, access-safe filters, hybrid fusion, changed-policy tests, clear abstention, and keyboard-readable evidence while staff remain the decision makers. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Public-Benefits Policy Navigator with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Inventory source owner, authority, location, version, time, license, access policy, checksum, parser, and lineage for every ingested document.
Detect or receive language and apply language- and script-appropriate word, subword, character, or morphological tokenization.
Apply BM25 term saturation and length normalization with measured parameters rather than unexplained defaults.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Rag Source Inventory Provenance
steps.instruction:
project case — A project RAG engineering team handles case 9528 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to fuse lexical and dense candidates without comparing uncalibrated raw scores; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: rag-reciprocal-rank-fusion-1whf2e
# Competency: rag-reciprocal-rank-fusion.
# Case 8502: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence.
def evidence_rag_reciprocal_rank_fusion_1whf2e(lexical_rank: int = 2, dense_rank: int = 5, rank_constant: int = 60, lexical_available: bool = True, dense_available: bool = True, calibrated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1whf2e = "1whf2e-e2fhw1-8502"
    assert evidence_variant_1whf2e
    evidence_axes_1whf2e = (
        "corpus1whf2e",
        "querye2fhw1",
        "tenant8502",
        "index1whf2e8502",
        "prompte2fhw18502",
        "evaluation85021whf2e",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1whf2e)) == 6
    if rank_constant <= 0 or lexical_rank < 1 or dense_rank < 1:
        return False, 0.0, "rank-input"
    if not lexical_available and not dense_available:
        return False, 0.0, "no-retriever"
    lexical_score = 1.0 / (rank_constant + lexical_rank) if lexical_available else 0.0
    dense_score = 1.0 / (rank_constant + dense_rank) if dense_available else 0.0
    fused = lexical_score + dense_score
    if not calibrated:
        return False, fused, "fusion-evaluation"
    assert fused > 0.0
    return True, round(fused, 6), "reciprocal-rank-fusion"
